title: Nature and the Environment Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The usual weather in a place over a long time.
rounds.explanation: climate: The usual weather in a place over a long time.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A very tall hill with steep sides.
rounds.explanation: mountain: A very tall hill with steep sides.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To go and look at new places.
rounds.explanation: explore: To go and look at new places.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: What the sky and air are like today.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A big area with many trees.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Power that helps things move or work.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The desert has a hot, dry climate.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: We saw white snow on top of the mountain.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which word completes the sentence: There are _____ trees in the forest.
rounds.explanation: Trees can be counted one by one, and countable nouns take many rather than much.

rounds.prompt: Which question word asks for a reason?
rounds.explanation: Why asks for the reason behind something, while where asks about place and when asks about time.

rounds.prompt: In “From Coast to Forest”, what did Nora find shining in the sand?
rounds.explanation: Nora first calls it a rock, then corrects herself and says it is metal.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: In “Our Wonderful Nature”, what does the sunshine do?
choices: Nature gives us water, trees and air.
She has only seen pictures of a forest.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: The sunshine warms the land.

rounds.prompt: The speaker gives three instructions before the trip. What are they?
choices: Nature gives us water, trees and air.
They visited the beach and coast, then the forest, then the mountain viewpoint.
Bring water, wear a hat, and write what you see.
rounds.answer: Bring water, wear a hat, and write what you see.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Bring water, wear a hat, and write what you see.

rounds.prompt: Which three places did the class visit on their nature trip?
choices: She found metal, though she first thought it was a rock.
They visited the beach and coast, then the forest, then the mountain viewpoint.
It asks us to take care of nature and to share it with everyone.
rounds.answer: They visited the beach and coast, then the forest, then the mountain viewpoint.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: They visited the beach and coast, then the forest, then the mountain viewpoint.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Describe a Place in Nature
Speaking 3 — Have You Ever…?
rounds.target: Have you ever explored a ______ (forest / beach / mountain)?

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 — Why We Protect the Planet
rounds.target: We must protect the planet because litter harms the crabs on the beach.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: I built sandcastles on the beach.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: We will explore the forest path tomorrow.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Water, rock, and air are all types of matter.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'forest' mean in this unit?
choices: A big area with many trees.
The usual weather in a place over a long time.
What the sky and air are like today.
rounds.answer: A big area with many trees.
rounds.explanation: forest: A big area with many trees.

rounds.prompt: What does 'energy' mean in this unit?
choices: The usual weather in a place over a long time.
What the sky and air are like today.
Power that helps things move or work.
rounds.answer: Power that helps things move or work.
rounds.explanation: energy: Power that helps things move or work.

rounds.prompt: What does 'weather' mean in this unit?
choices: How hot or cold something is.
What the sky and air are like today.
The usual weather in a place over a long time.
rounds.answer: What the sky and air are like today.
rounds.explanation: weather: What the sky and air are like today.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'coast'?
rounds.explanation: coast is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'metal'?
rounds.explanation: metal is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'sunshine'?
rounds.explanation: sunshine is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: How hot or cold something is.
The light and warmth that come from the sun.
A very tall hill with steep sides.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: Land with sand near the sea.
The land next to the sea.
To go and look at new places.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: What everything is made of; it takes up space.
A hard, shiny material used to make things.
The usual weather in a place over a long time.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Which is the correct short answer to “Have you explored a forest?”
choices: Yes, I did explore a forest today.
rounds.explanation: A short answer repeats the helping verb from the question, and that question begins with have.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence is an imperative that tells someone what to do?
choices: We are protecting the planet every day.
rounds.explanation: An imperative starts with the base verb and needs no subject, because the word you is understood.

rounds.prompt: What is the past participle of the verb “freeze”?
rounds.explanation: Freeze is irregular: froze is the past simple, but after have or has we need frozen.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: What does the word “climate” mean?
choices: Everything in the world is made of it, and it takes up space.
The usual weather in a place over a long time.
A big ball in space, like Earth or Mars.
Power that helps things move or work.
rounds.answer: The usual weather in a place over a long time.
rounds.explanation: Climate describes the usual weather across many years, which is why the desert has a hot, dry climate.

rounds.prompt: In the story, the sunshine was bright and the _____ was hot.
rounds.explanation: Weather is what the sky and air are like on that one day, so it fits a single hot morning at the beach.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence uses the present perfect correctly?
choices: She has saw the mountain from the beach.
rounds.explanation: She takes has, and see becomes the past participle seen, so only the first sentence is complete and correct.
